purpose: Define the reviewer roles, tracks, and phases for the first U.S. sector-regime depth wave.

reviewTracks.goal: Challenge HIPAA privacy and breach mappings against real regulated-health workflow expectations.
Challenge GLBA and NYDFS overlap claims against financial-sector security and governance expectations.
Challenge CCPA/CPRA rights, retention, and service-provider mappings against California privacy-law practice.
Challenge whether the mapped controls are actually narrow enough to be trustworthy and testable.

reviewerRoles.description: Reviews HIPAA Privacy and Breach mappings for health-law accuracy and practical notice obligations.
Reviews whether the mapped HIPAA controls correspond to real health-ops workflows rather than abstract policy prose.
Reviews GLBA and NYDFS anchors, scoping assumptions, and notice/governance boundaries.
Reviews whether the mapped GLBA and NYDFS controls are operationally meaningful in real financial-sector systems.
Reviews CCPA/CPRA rights, service-provider, and retention mappings against California privacy-law practice.
Reviews whether the public control layer is precise enough to support rights handling and retention evidence.
Reviews the technical plausibility of the machine-checkable sector controls.
Reviews whether the claimed decidable subset is narrow enough to stay honest when moved into typed or Lean-backed verification.

phases.goal: Confirm that published exact anchors and relation semantics are defensible.
passCriteria: Each published anchor has an authoritative public source and a narrow relation statement.
No documented objection says a published exact anchor overclaims the current control.

phases.goal: Confirm that the mapped public controls are narrow enough to support proof, attestation, or judgment routing.
passCriteria: Each reviewed control has an explicit route and a credible evidence story.
No reviewed control silently hides a rights-heavy or judgment-heavy obligation behind a proof-like label.

phases.goal: Confirm that fixture-backed public examples tell the truth about what is and is not shown.
passCriteria: Trust-surface reports do not imply full HIPAA, GLBA, NYDFS, or CCPA compliance from narrow example corridors.
Remaining gaps and judgment layers are visible to external readers.

remainingBlockers: Run the first external review round with health, financial-regulation, and California privacy specialists.
Promote the next typed claim schemas and fixture-backed controls for these regimes before claiming broader depth.
